content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • •• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 14, 1977

Honorable Arlen Gregorio
The State Senate
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Opinion Request
No. 75-092

Dear Senator Gregorio:

On March 31, 1975, you asked the Fair Political Practices Commission for a formal opinion interpreting the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act. Subsequently, your opinion request was consolidated with those received from several other California officials. On August 3, 1976, the Commission adopted an opinion, 2 FPPC Ops. 110, in which it answered all of the questions posed by the various requests, except those concerning the applicability of the mass mailing provisions of Government Code Section 84305 to certain mailings made on behalf of legislators at public expense.

Although the Commission has indicated that certain types of government expenditures are reportable under the Political Reform Act as contributions and/or expenditures, precise guidelines outlining reporting obligations in the context of publicly financed mass mailings, and hence the applicability of Government Code Section 84305 to such mailings, have not yet been promulgated. However, the staff presently is researching and drafting regulations that will establish the necessary guidelines, and these regulations will be responsive to the question posed in your opinion request. Because the regulations will address the issue posed in your opinion request, the Commission decided, at its meeting on March 2, 1977, that a formal opinion would not be necessary and that your opinion request should be dismissed. Accordingly, no formal opinion will be issued in response to your request.

When drafts of the proposed regulations are available, copies will be provided to you for your comments and suggestions. Any comments should be directed to Ted Prim, an attorney on the Commission staff.

Honorable Arlen Gregorio
March 14, 1977
Page Two


     I apologize for the long delay in answering your
opinion request.  If you have any further questions, please
feel free to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director

MB:plh

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the mass mailing provisions of Government Code Section 84305 apply to certain mailings made on behalf of legislators at public expense and what reporting obligations exist for publicly financed mass mailings.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Commission determined that a formal opinion would not be necessary at this time because staff is researching and drafting regulations that will establish guidelines addressing the applicability of the mass mailing provisions to publicly financed mailings, and those regulations will be responsive to the question posed.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a declination letter (Opinion Request No. 75-092). Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; instead contains explanation that regulations in progress will address the issue. OCR quality appears good overall with minor formatting variations.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to issue a formal opinion on whether Government Code Section 84305's mass mailing provisions apply to certain publicly financed legislative mailings, instead indicating that staff is drafting regulations that will address these reporting obligations and applicability issues.
